repo_prior_art.cite: none; API §3 reports no completed PartialID entry, and pid_manski1990test is unrelated

published_prior_art.cite: LannersRudinVolfovskyParikh2025, §4/Lemma 1/Remark 1 already gives the pointwise overlap conditions and an average-overlap compatibility test, but not a stated common-latent-extension iff

MastenPoirier2020 supplies directional-delta/numerical-bootstrap uniform frontier inference; FangSantos2019 and HongLi2018 supply the nonsmooth-resampling ingredients, but none states this learned-nuisance exact-compatibility-set result

novelty_flags.label: def:published-endpoints; prop:two-stratum-cancellation; oeq:exact-compatibility
novelty_flags.one_line: LannersRudinVolfovskyParikh2025 defines its lower sensitivity endpoints as (1-γ)μ_{1t} and (1-ρ)μ_{0t} (Appendix B.1, pp. 23–24), whereas the core calls μ/(1+γ) and μ/(1+ρ) its published endpoints; thus its G^{LRVP} calculation is not the cited paper's proxy and the current letter-tier kernel is below novelty_target=field until this comparator is repaired.

soundness_flags.label: ass:relative-nuc
soundness_flags.one_line: Tagged as Lanners' standard model, but LannersRudinVolfovskyParikh2025 uses [(1-ρ)μ_{0t},(1+ρ)μ_{0t}], not [μ_{0t}/(1+ρ),(1+ρ)μ_{0t}]; either use the published model or retag and justify a distinct ratio model.

soundness_flags.label: ass:study-exchangeability
soundness_flags.one_line: Tagged as Lanners' standard model, but the cited paper uses [(1-γ)μ_{1t},(1+γ)μ_{1t}], not [μ_{1t}/(1+γ),(1+γ)μ_{1t}].

soundness_flags.label: ass:trial-randomization
soundness_flags.one_line: The cited Lanners A3 is joint randomization, (Y(0),Y(1)) ⟂ A | X,S=1; the core states only marginal independence Y(t) ⟂ A for each t, so the published-model comparison is not aligned.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace both reciprocal lower endpoints by Lanners' actual (1-ρ)μ and (1-γ)μ endpoints, impose the corresponding parameter domain/support clipping, and recompute a nondegenerate cancellation witness for the actual G_t test.

After aligning the model, make the strict comparison explicit: prove C_P=0 iff a common extension exists and prove, over a stated nondegenerate class, that G_t≥0 can hold while C_P>0; then derive the resulting sharp compatibility region for the published sensitivity model.

Turn Open-ended Question 2 into a separate contribution with an explicit Hölder-driven separation rate and a theorem-level comparison to Masten–Poirier's grid-frontier inference and Lanners' fixed-nuisance resampling.

recommended_next_step: Revise the sensitivity model and all “published proxy” formulas to match Lanners et al.; then rerun the cancellation calculation and reassess whether the exact-extension plus inference package reaches the field floor.
